candidate_problems.text: Can 'counterfactual structure' be made precise in a non-circular way that distinguishes explanation from mere predictive compression across domains? The conjecture hinges on preservation of counterfactuals, but it leaves open what counts as the relevant variation space, how to individuate laws/mechanisms versus accidents, and how to test preservation without already presupposing explanatory understanding. Pushed to the limit, this becomes the problem of whether there exists a domain-general criterion for explanatory compression, or only family-resemblances across physics, biology, economics, and mathematics.

Is there really a constrained optimum between label-like compression and archive-like storage, or is the tradeoff structure fundamentally plural and observer-relative? The conjecture posits a narrow band where explanation lives, but it is unresolved whether this band is objective, unique, and world-imposed, or whether it depends on purposes, intervention sets, computational limits, and background knowledge. At the limit, one must ask whether 'best explanation' is a property of the world’s structure or of an agent-system interface.

What separates explanatory necessity from merely successful compact derivation, especially in mathematics and highly expressive models? The conjecture claims some proofs and models compress without explaining, but it does not yet identify the load-bearing feature that makes one derivation reveal 'why' while another is only a trick. Pushed hard, this opens the problem of whether explanatory depth can be characterized by dependency-preservation, robustness under reformulation, and modal reach, or whether 'why' is irreducibly pragmatic.
